articles.title: SPEND $1 TODAY, SAVE UP TO $2K ON YOUR NEXT VACATION
articles.summary: Ready to save up to $2,000 on every vacation? Dollar Flight Club has your back—TRY THE CLUB FOR JUST $1 IF YOU SIGN UP IN THE NEXT 12 HOURS [4]. That means; Hawaii from $174, Iceland from $215, or Greece from $245 round trip. Join over 1 million members saving on travel this year.

articles.title: APPLE'S LONG-AWAITED ‘HOMEACCESSORY' COULD HAVE SQUARE DISPLAY, A18 CHIP FOR AI, MORE
articles.summary: Details about Apple's rumored new home accessory have been leaked. The device, codenamed J490, combines the capabilities of the iPad, Apple TV, and HomePod. It will be powered by the A18 chip, presumably to support Apple Intelligence features. The device currently has a squarish display - it is unclear whether the square aspect ratio will make it to the final product. It has a built-in camera that works for FaceTime and other video conferencing apps. The camera can identify hand gestures from a distance and identify users to enable personal requests. More details about the device are available in the article.

articles.title: STARLINK HITS 4 MILLION SUBSCRIBERS
articles.summary: SpaceX's Starlink satellite network is expected to pass four million customers this week. The company has gained around a million new customers since the end of May. It started providing beta service of its product in October 2020, hitting one million subscribers in December 2022. Its constellation now comprises nearly 6,000 satellites, which service companies and individuals in nearly 100 countries.

articles.title: STEM CELLS REVERSE WOMAN'S DIABETES — A WORLD FIRST
articles.summary: A 25-year-old woman with type 1 diabetes has started producing her own insulin less than three months after receiving a transplant of reprogrammed stem cells. A year after the transplant, she can now eat sugar and enjoy eating everything. The surgery has completely reversed diabetes in the patient, who previously required substantial amounts of insulin. While the results are positive, they still need to be replicated in more people, and the researchers want to wait five more years before considering the patient as 'cured'.

articles.title: RADIAN AEROSPACE COMPLETES GROUND TESTS OF PROTOTYPE SPACE PLANE
articles.summary: Radian Aerospace has announced the completion of a series of ground tests for its reusable space plane, which will be able to take off from airfields and land on runways like conventional airplanes. The tests were completed with a subscale prototype flight vehicle. They aimed to generate data on how the vehicle would fly and handle and to compare the data to simulations. While the vehicle did not fly, it did perform a series of small hops. The prototype vehicle is much smaller than the final vehicle, but the data will help inform key pieces of the final design and flight control systems.

articles.title: POSTGRESQL 17 RELEASED!
articles.summary: PostgreSQL 17 improves on performance and scalability while adapting to emergent data access and storage patterns. It adds significant overall performance gains, including an overhauled memory management implementation for vacuum, optimizations to storage access and improvements for high concurrency workloads, speedups in bulk loading and exports, and query execution improvements for indexes. It also has features that benefit brand-new workloads and critical systems. This article covers the highlights of the new release.

articles.title: HOTWIRE NATIVE
articles.summary: Hotwire Native is a web-first framework for building native mobile apps. It enables developers to build screens in HTML and CSS and reuse them across every platform. Hotwire Native provides developers with full access to underlying iOS and Android SDKs and APIs as soon as they're released. It allows small teams to build highly functional, beautiful, and sustainable mobile apps without the headache and rigmarole of traditional native development.

articles.title: WORDPRESS.ORG BANS WP ENGINE, BLOCKS IT FROM ACCESSING ITS RESOURCES
articles.summary: WordPress.org has banned WP Engine from accessing its resources. The ban prevents WP Engine customers from accessing security updates, leaving them vulnerable. WP Engine is now working on fixes. The company claims that WordPress co-creator Matt Mullenweg is misusing his control of WordPress to interfere with WP Engine's customers' access to WordPress.org.

articles.title: CRITICAL MASS AND TIPPING POINTS: HOW TO IDENTIFY INFLECTION POINTS BEFORE THEY HAPPEN
articles.summary: The concept of critical mass can be used to understand everything from viral cat videos to why changing habits is so hard. Sometimes, it can seem as if drastic changes happen at random, but there is a logic to these occurrences that can be explained by the concept of critical mass - the point at which something is prevalent enough to grow or sustain a process, reaction, or technology. The concept can help us understand the world around us by letting us spot changes before they occur. A firm understanding can help give people an edge in launching products, changing habits, and choosing investments.

articles.title: 👩🎓 SOUTHERN NEW HAMPSHIRE UNIVERSITY IS OFFERING 200+ FLEXIBLE ONLINE DEGREES AT AFFORDABLE TUITION RATES
articles.summary: You don't need to put your career on pause to get a high-quality education. Browse 200+ affordable, online degree programs at SNHU — an accredited, nonprofit institution. Start here (US learners only)

articles.title: TAILS OS JOINS FORCES WITH TOR PROJECT IN MERGER
GIT ABSORB
articles.summary: git absorb is a port of Facebook's hg absorb, which is a workflow enhancement to version control that automatically folds uncommitted modifications into the appropriate draft ancestor change sets.

articles.title: OPENAI IN THROES OF EXECUTIVE EXODUS AS THREE WALK AT ONCE
articles.summary: OpenAI's Chief Technology Officer Mira Murati, Chief Research Officer Bob McGrew, and Research VP Barret Zoph are leaving the company.

articles.title: BIGGER AI CHATBOTS MORE INCLINED TO SPEW NONSENSE — AND PEOPLE DON'T ALWAYS REALIZE
articles.summary: The bigger versions of three major artificial intelligence chatbots are more inclined to generate wrong answers rather than admit ignorance - people aren't great at spotting the bad answers.

articles.title: REWRITING RUST
articles.summary: The Rust programming language feels like a first-generation product - progress on the language has slowed, with the Rust RFC process now a graveyard of good ideas.

articles.title: AN INTERVIEW WITH META CTO ANDREW BOSWORTH ABOUT ORION AND REALITY LABS
articles.summary: In this interview, Meta's Chief Technical Officer Andrew Bosworth, who created Facebook's News Feed, built out its ad product, and leads Meta Reality Labs, talks about his history at Meta, Meta's Orion smart glasses, and the competition between Apple and Meta in the space.
